title: TAVR-VLM: Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding for Hallucination-Resistant Report Generation
abstract: arXiv:2606.26874v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR) planning requires meticulous multimodal reasoning. However, adapting Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to this high-stakes domain is severely impeded by diagnostic hallucinations, where generated text lacks anatomical grounding. To address this, TAVR-VLM is introduced: a novel framework featuring Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding Attention (R-CGA) that instantiates a model-internal ``Risk $\rightarrow$ Region $\rightarrow$ Word'' structural grounding pathway. R-CGA compresses multimodal inputs into a causal risk bottleneck, purifying dense visual features into a global risk mask. During autoregressive generation, a support-projected causal consistency objective constrains token-level grounding within the risk-defined support mask. Evaluated on $\text{M}^3\text{TAVR}$, a comprehensive 1,482-patient cohort, TAVR-VLM establishes a new state-of-the-art. It achieves an AUROC of 0.896, boost

Title: TAVR-VLM: Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding for Hallucination-Resistant Report Generation
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26874v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR) planning requires meticulous multimodal reasoning. However, adapting Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to this high-stakes domain is severely impeded by diagnostic hallucinations, where generated text lacks anatomical grounding. To address this, TAVR-VLM is introduced: a novel framework featuring Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding Attention (R-CGA) that instantiates a model-internal ``Risk $\rightarrow$ Region $\rightarrow$ Word'' structural grounding pathway. R-CGA compresses multimodal inputs into a causal risk bottleneck, purifying dense visual features into a global risk mask. During autoregressive generation, a support-projected causal consistency objective constrains token-level grounding within the risk-defined support mask. Evaluated on $\text{M}^3\text{TAVR}$, a comprehensive 1,482-patient cohort, TAVR-VLM establishes a new state-of-the-art. It achieves an AUROC of 0.896, boost
translated_title: TAVR-VLM：基于风险条件因果约束的抗幻觉报告生成方法
translated_abstract: 经导管主动脉瓣置换术（TAVR）规划需要细致的多模态推理。然而，将多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）应用于这一高风险领域时，会因诊断性幻觉（即生成的文本缺乏解剖学依据）而严重受阻。为此，本文提出TAVR-VLM框架，其核心是风险条件因果约束注意力（R-CGA），该机制构建了一条模型内部的“风险→区域→词汇”结构因果约束路径。R-CGA将多模态输入压缩为因果风险瓶颈，将稠密视觉特征纯化为全局风险掩码。在自回归生成过程中，通过支持投影因果一致性目标，将词级约束限制在风险定义的支撑掩码内。在包含1482名患者的综合数据集M³TAVR上评估，TAVR-VLM达到了新的最优水平，AUROC为0.896。
tldr: 本文提出TAVR-VLM框架，通过风险条件因果约束注意力机制构建“风险→区域→词汇”的结构因果路径，有效缓解了多模态大模型在TAVR规划中的诊断幻觉问题，在1482名患者数据集上取得了新的最优结果（AUROC 0.896）。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes TAVR-VLM, a framework that introduces Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding Attention (R-CGA) to build a "Risk → Region → Word" structural causal pathway, effectively mitigating diagnostic hallucinations in MLLMs for TAVR planning, achieving a new state-of-the-art (AUROC 0.896) on a 1,482-patient cohort.
